prose: Fukui reactivity indices for phenol (13 atoms) were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (PySCF RKS, tier 'standard'), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Exact four-center integrals (no density fitting). Partial charges via Mulliken scheme. The neutral (N), cation (N−1, doublet), and anion (N+1, doublet) SCFs all converged. Integrity status: OK, trustworthy=true. No engine warnings. Charge conservation checks passed (sum f+ = sum f- = 1.0000). The most electrophilic atom is C (atom 3, 1-based), f+ = 2.1028319. The most nucleophilic atom is O (atom 1, 1-based), f- = 0.16201477. The dual descriptor identifies atom 3 (C) as the hardest electrophilic centre (+2.0319) and atom 1 (O) as the hardest nucleophilic centre (−1.1572), consistent with phenol's known chemistry (OH as electrophile-attack site; ortho/para carbons as nucleophilic-attack sites in electrophilic aromatic substitution).
